name: codebase-memory-mcp
description: High-performance code intelligence MCP server. Indexes codebases into a persistent knowledge graph — average repo in milliseconds. 158 languages, sub-ms queries, 99% fewer tokens. Single static binary, zero dependencies.

curation.summary: Codebase Memory MCP is a high-performance code intelligence server that indexes entire codebases into a persistent knowledge graph using tree-sitter AST parsing and hybrid LSP semantic resolution. It provides sub-millisecond structural queries for AI coding agents, supporting 158 languages with a single static binary and zero dependencies.
curation.whyNotable: This project achieves full codebase indexing in milliseconds (Linux kernel in 3 minutes) with 99% fewer tokens than file-by-file exploration, backed by published research and rigorous security certifications (SLSA 3, OpenSSF Scorecard). Its hybrid LSP approach for 10 languages and compatibility with 11 coding agents make it a uniquely practical and efficient tool for AI-assisted development.
strengths: Extremely fast indexing (milliseconds for average repos) and sub-millisecond queries
Supports 158 languages via tree-sitter with hybrid LSP for 10 major languages
Single static binary with zero dependencies, easy install across macOS/Linux/Windows
Published research paper with benchmarks showing 83% answer quality and 10x fewer tokens
High production readiness: 35 releases, CI, 5604 tests, SLSA 3, VirusTotal scanning
limitations: Limited to code structure analysis (no runtime behavior or dynamic analysis)
Hybrid LSP only covers 10 languages, leaving 148 languages with basic AST only
No Docker support or examples provided in the repository
useCases: AI coding agents needing fast, token-efficient codebase understanding
Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Aider, or Gemini CLI for code navigation
Large-scale codebase analysis and refactoring with minimal latency
curation.targetAudience: Developers and teams using AI coding agents who need rapid, efficient codebase exploration and structural querying
curation.oneLiner: The fastest code intelligence MCP server that indexes entire codebases into a knowledge graph in milliseconds for AI coding agents.

name: no-mistakes

curation.summary: no-mistakes is a Go-based local git proxy that intercepts pushes, runs an AI-driven validation pipeline in an isolated worktree, and only forwards the branch to the remote after all checks pass, automatically opening a clean PR. It is agent-agnostic, supports multiple AI coding agents, and keeps the human in the loop for any non-trivial fixes.
curation.whyNotable: It introduces a novel workflow that integrates AI validation directly into the git push process, ensuring only clean, reviewed code reaches the remote. Its agent-agnostic design and non-blocking worktree approach make it a practical tool for teams adopting AI-assisted development.
strengths: Non-blocking isolated worktree keeps developer's work uninterrupted
Agent-agnostic, supporting multiple AI coding agents
Automated clean PR creation with AI-driven validation pipeline
Human-in-the-loop for safe fixes and escalations
limitations: No tests included, reducing confidence in reliability
No Docker support, limiting deployment flexibility
Documentation lacks examples and detailed configuration guidance
useCases: Automated code review and validation before pushing to remote
Integrating AI agents into CI/CD pipelines for quality gates
Enforcing code quality standards across team contributions
curation.targetAudience: Developers and teams using AI coding agents who want to automate code review and ensure clean PRs without disrupting their workflow
curation.oneLiner: A local git proxy that runs AI-driven validation in an isolated worktree and only pushes clean code with automatic PR creation.

name: agent-native

curation.summary: Agent-Native is an open-source TypeScript framework for building agent-native applications where agents operate inside real apps, not just alongside them. curation.whyNotable: It introduces a novel 'define once, use everywhere' paradigm for agent actions, bridging the gap between traditional app development and agentic workflows with a unified runtime. The project has rapid release velocity (100 releases in 6 months) and offers complete, cloneable SaaS templates, making it highly practical for production use.

Rich set of built-in primitives: shared actions, SQL state, identity, tools, skills, jobs, observability
Complete, cloneable SaaS templates for rapid prototyping
High release frequency and active development with 38 contributors

Creating coding agents that can plan, build, and review code with visual feedback

name: thunderbird-android
description: Thunderbird for Android – Open Source Email App for Android (fka K-9 Mail)

curation.summary: Thunderbird for Android is a powerful, privacy-focused open-source email app for Android, based on K-9 Mail. It supports multiple email accounts, a unified inbox, and is available on Google Play, F-Droid, and GitHub. The project is actively maintained by Mozilla and a global community.
curation.whyNotable: This project is notable as the official Thunderbird email client for Android, bringing Mozilla's privacy-first approach to mobile email. It has a large user base, frequent releases, and strong community support, making it a key player in open-source communication tools.
strengths: Strong privacy focus with no data monetization
Active development with frequent releases (33 in 6 months)
Large community with 441 contributors and 13.6k stars
limitations: No automated tests detected in the repository
High number of open issues (1,005) may indicate maintenance challenges
Limited to Android platform only
useCases: Managing multiple email accounts from a single app
Privacy-conscious email communication on Android
Open-source alternative to proprietary email clients
curation.targetAudience: Android users seeking a privacy-respecting, feature-rich email client, especially those already using Thunderbird on desktop.
curation.oneLiner: Thunderbird for Android is the official, privacy-first email client for Android, built on the legacy of K-9 Mail.
